skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: A set of resources to help write internal communications using company-preferred formats including status reports, leadership updates, newsletters, FAQs, and incident reports
skill.summary: Templates and guidelines for writing professional internal communications in standardized company formats
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 15 static analysis findings are false positives. LICENSE.txt contains standard Apache 2.0 legal text with URL references that triggered pattern matching. SKILL.md contains markdown documentation describing file organization, not executable code. No actual network calls, command execution, or cryptographic operations exist in this documentation-only skill.

content.value_statement: Teams struggle with inconsistent internal communication formats. This skill provides standardized templates for status reports, newsletters, FAQs, and leadership updates that match your company's preferred style.
actual_capabilities: Generate 3P updates (Progress, Plans, Problems) in standardized format
Draft company newsletter content with appropriate tone and structure
Create FAQ responses using established answer templates
Write status reports for leadership and stakeholders
Format incident reports with proper sections and timelines
Produce project updates aligned with company communication standards
limitations: Requires existing guideline files in examples/ directory to function
Cannot create new communication formats without provided templates
Relies on user to provide specific content details and context
Does not automatically distribute or publish communications

use_cases.title: Team Lead Weekly Reporting
use_cases.description: Generate consistent 3P updates (Progress, Plans, Problems) for leadership review without spending time on formatting

use_cases.title: Internal Communications Team
use_cases.description: Scale company-wide newsletter production by using standardized templates for consistent brand voice

use_cases.title: Incident Response Documentation
use_cases.description: Quickly produce properly formatted incident reports during post-mortem processes

prompt_templates.title: Basic status report
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a weekly status report for my engineering team covering: completed work on feature X, next week's plan to start testing, and a blocker waiting on API access from the platform team

prompt_templates.title: 3P update format
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3P update using the standard template. Progress: launched v2 dashboard. Plans: roll out to beta users next sprint. Problems: need design review for mobile layout

prompt_templates.title: Newsletter section draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft the Engineering section for this month's company newsletter. Include: new hire onboarding, completion of the migration project, and upcoming hackathon announcement

prompt_templates.title: Incident report documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an incident report for the production outage on January 15. Timeline: detected 14:32 UTC, mitigated 15:47 UTC, resolved 16:20 UTC. Root cause: database connection pool exhaustion. Action items: increase pool size, add monitoring alerts

output_examples.input: Write a 3P update: finished user authentication refactor, planning to start performance optimization next week, blocked on getting AWS credentials
output_examples.output:
Weekly 3P Update

**Progress:**
- Completed user authentication refactor, improving code maintainability and security

**Plans:**
- Begin performance optimization initiative focusing on page load times and database query efficiency

**Problems:**
- Awaiting AWS credentials access to proceed with infrastructure-related tasks

output_examples.input: Create an FAQ response for: How do I request time off?
output_examples.output:
FAQ: How do I request time off?

To request time off:

1. Submit your request through the HR portal at least 2 weeks in advance for planned time off
2. Notify your direct manager via email or chat
3. Update your team calendar with your out-of-office dates
4. Ensure coverage for any critical responsibilities during your absence

For emergency time off, contact your manager directly as soon as possible.

best_practices: Always identify the communication type before selecting a template format
Include specific metrics and concrete details rather than vague statements
Review generated content for accuracy before sending to stakeholders
anti_patterns: Using newsletter templates for formal incident reports
Omitting timeline details in status updates and incident reports
Sending AI-generated communications without human review for sensitive topics

faq.question: What types of internal communications can this skill help me write?
faq.answer: This skill supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports using your company's preferred formats.

faq.question: Do I need to provide the guideline files?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires guideline files in the examples/ directory (3p-updates.md, company-newsletter.md, faq-answers.md, general-comms.md) to function properly.

faq.question: Can this skill create new communication formats?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses existing templates. For new formats not covered by current guidelines, you will need to provide the format specifications or ask for clarification.

faq.question: Is the generated content ready to send without editing?
faq.answer: Generated content should always be reviewed before distribution. Add specific details, verify accuracy, and ensure the tone matches your audience and company culture.

faq.question: Which Claude tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All three can access the guideline files and generate communications using the provided templates.

faq.question: How do I handle communications that don't match existing templates?
faq.answer: For communications that don't match existing guidelines, use the general-comms.md template or ask for clarification about the desired format before proceeding.
